I prefer risk-syncing baskets of accounts. Usually I have 10–20 accounts in rotation, copying sets of 5 across each firm (Apex excluded since you can trade 5–20 at once). I like spreading risk this way because it lets me exploit one of the biggest edges in prop trading: I can be red on one set of accounts and green on another the same day.  That keeps me more level-headed on red days and helps limit big drawdowns on the inevitable days where I’m just not seeing it clearly or end up overtrading — which happens to all of us. I still get the leverage of copy trading and the ability to secure 5-figure payouts, but my entire result isn’t tied to one emotional or poorly executed day. That’s a unique edge with props that you don’t really have trading personal cash, so I make sure to use it

The largest crypto hack in history just happened — $1.4B drained from Bybit's cold wallet by North Korea's Lazarus Group. Let that sink in. A cold wallet, designed to be offline and untouchable, was compromised through a sophisticated supply chain attack targeting Safe{Wallet} infrastructure.  However, what's remarkable isn't just the scale — it's the method. Lazarus didn't brute-force anything. They manipulated the signing interface itself, tricking @Bybit's team into approving malicious transactions. @SlowMist_Team's forensic breakdown confirmed the attack vector with chilling clarity. Moreover, this exposes a brutal truth: multisig and cold storage alone aren't enough if your transaction layer is poisoned.  Furthermore, this is a wake-up call for every protocol, exchange, and dev team. Hardware isolation means nothing without end-to-end verification of your signing pipeline. We need open-source, auditable UI layers for transaction signing — yesterday.  What security changes is your team making right now? 왜 금방 없어질 가능성이 큰가?  테슬라의 OTA 업데이트 방식: 테슬라는 FSD(자율주행) 관련 보안·소프트웨어 잠금을 매우 적극적으로 막습니다. 과거에도 CAN 버스 조작, 서드파티 장치, 지역 제한 우회 등을 OTA로 빠르게 차단한 전례가 많아요. 이번 해킹의 취약점: 장치가 CAN 버스 신호를 “스푸핑(spoofing)”하는 방식이라, 테슬라가 FSD 소프트웨어에 추가 검증 로직이나 CAN 프레임 체크를 넣는 업데이트만 하면 끝납니다. (이미 TACC 체크를 우회하는 코드를 개발자가 숨겼지만, 테슬라는 더 깊은 레벨에서 감지할 수 있어요.) 현재 상황 (2026년 3월 29일 기준): 아직 테슬라가 공식적으로 차단하지 않았지만, 이 해킹이 한국·유럽·일본 테슬라 커뮤니티에서 급속도로 퍼지고 있어요. 테슬라는 이런 “대규모 바이럴”을 감지하면 즉시 OTA를 밀어붙이는 패턴입니다. (보통 1~4주 안에 첫 패치가 나오는 경우가 많음)  다만 완전히 사라지진 않을 수도 있어요  개발자(Michał Gapiński, Tesla Android)는 새로운 버전을 빠르게 내놓을 가능성이 큽니다. (과거에도 테슬라 업데이트 후 1~2주 만에 대응한 적 있음) 장치가 “플러그 앤 플레이”라서, 업데이트 후에도 다시 연결하면 작동할 수 있지만, 테슬라가 차량을 “벽돌(brick)”하거나 보증을 무효화할 위험은 커집니다.  결론: 단기적으로(며칠~몇 주 내) 테슬라 OTA로 막힐 확률이 압도적으로 높아요. 이미 FSD v14.2를 비공식으로 돌리는 영상이 퍼지고 있지만, 테슬라는 이런 걸 그냥 둘 스타일이 절대 아니에요. 사용 중이시라면 지금 당장 안전을 위해 빼는 걸 추천합니다. (법적·안전·보증 문제도 크니까요.)

1번  보이콧에서 확실하게 하려면 국민저항권 발동이 조건이 되어야 되요. 2번은 보이콧 한다면 부정선거 척결이 무엇보다 중요하겠죠? 3번은 화웨이는 사이버(온라인)공격 방식인데, 한국은 온라인과 오프라인 구분이 무의미할정도로 선거 조작이 대놓고 진행되고 있고 이미 너무 많은 사례가 나와버렸어요. 그리고 부정선거가 있으려면 부정선거 행위와 무관하게 투표 행위가 있어야 되요.  최소 모든 국민들의 90% 이상의 인원이 부정선거를 인지해서 보이콧을 해야 성립하는데 아직도 모르쇠하는 경우가 많은것도 문제에요. 아무리 화웨이에서 사이버 활동의 부정선거 수단으로 이용되고 있어도 오프라인에서  선거 무효가 되버리면 무용지물이니까 말이죠.

[영국의 국가 사이버 보안 전략] 영국의 부총리인 올리버 다우든(보수당)이 2024년 3월 영국 의회에서 발언하는 영상입니다. (올리버 다우든: 보수당 소속 하원의원, 당시 영국 부총리(2023–2024) 신분도 겸임.)  "국가 사이버보안 전략을 통해 우리는 2025년까지 핵심 국가 기반시설의 사이버 회복력을 높이기 위해 26억 파운드(원화로 약 4조 5천억 원)를 투자하고 있으며, 우리 디지털 환경의 가장 중요한 부분을 국가 및 비국가 행위자들에게 더 어려운 공격 대상으로 만들고 있습니다."  "정부는 중국이 영국의 안보와 가치에 제기하는 구조적 도전에 대응하기 위해 필요한 도구, 전문성, 지식을 계속 구축하고 있습니다."  "2023년 통합 검토 업데이트는 이를 위한 조치를 취했으며, 정부 전반 프로그램에 대한 자금을 두 배로 늘리고, 중국어 교육에 대한 투자와 외교 전문성 강화를 포함했습니다." F-16 Fighter Jets Scrambled, Flares Deployed After Civilian Aircraft Violates Restricted Airspace Near Trump's Air Force One at Palm Beach Airport  A security incident at Palm Beach International Airport (PBI) on Sunday, March 29, 2026, prompted the rapid response of U.S. Air Force F-16 fighter jets, which deployed flares to gain the attention of a civilian pilot who had violated a Temporary Flight Restriction (TFR) and lost radio contact with air traffic."  The breach occurred at approximately 1:15 p.m. EDT, hours before President Donald Trump was scheduled to depart from PBI aboard Air Force One for Washington, D.C. Air Force One remained on the tarmac at the time, though the president was reportedly at his golf course in the area. A brief ground stop was imposed at the airport while authorities addressed the situation.  According to a statement from the North American Aerospace Defense Command (NORAD), shared via the Defense Visual Information Distribution Service: “The civilian aircraft violated the Temporary Flight Restriction (TFR) at approximately 1:15 p.m. Eastern Daylight Time. The aircraft was safely escorted out of the area by NORAD aircraft. During the intercept, NORAD aircraft dispensed flares, which may have been visible to the public.”  NORAD emphasized that the flares were used solely “to draw attention from or communicate with the pilot.” They burn out quickly and completely, posing no danger to people or property on the ground. The civilian plane, described as a general aviation aircraft, was safely escorted out of the restricted zone, and the situation was resolved without further incident.  Initial reports circulating on social media and from some pilots referenced a possible drone incursion or suspicious object, which led to helicopters being scrambled for investigation. However, the White House and officials later clarified that the primary issue involved a private plane that lost communications with the tower, not a drone threat or any direct risk to Air Force One or the president.  The White House confirmed there was no threat to the president or his aircraft. President Trump departed South Florida later that evening as planned.  Temporary Flight Restrictions are routinely established around the president’s locations, including Mar-a-Lago and PBI, to enhance security. NORAD and the U.S. military maintain a heightened state of readiness for airspace violations, especially in sensitive areas. Similar intercepts have occurred in the past near Palm Beach, with F-16s occasionally using flares during communications failures.  No injuries or damage were reported, and airport operations resumed shortly after the ground stop was lifted. The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) and local authorities have not released details about the civilian pilot or any potential enforcement actions as of Monday, March 30, 2026.  This incident underscores the rigorous protocols in place to protect presidential movements and maintain safe airspace around major airports and restricted zones.  This article draws from official NORAD statements, reporting by the New York Post, and multiple news outlets covering the event.

先日GitHubに流出したiOS向けエクスプロイトキット「DarkSword」を、ロシアFSB系APTグループTA446（ColdRiver / Star Blizzard）がスピアフィッシングに転用し始めていることが報告されています。 TA446がiCloudアカウントやAppleデバイスを標的にした活動はこれまで観測されておらず、流出をきっかけにiOS攻撃へ踏み出した初めてのケースとのこと。  偽メールはAtlantic Council（米シンクタンク）の討論会招待を装っており、リンクをクリックしたiPhoneユーザーだけがエクスプロイトキットに誘導される仕組み。iPhone以外のアクセスには無害なPDFが返されるサーバーサイドフィルタリングが用いられている模様です。  TA446は従来、パスワード付きZIPにバックドアMAYBEROBOTを同梱する手口が中心でしたが、今回はリンク型の配布への切り替えが見られています。 TA446はUNC6353とは別のアクターであることが確認されており、先日のポストで紹介したDarkSwordの流出から間もなく、国家系アクターによる実戦投入が報告された形です。  標的の幅も従来より広がっており、政府機関・シンクタンク・高等教育・金融・法律関連の組織が含まれているとのこと。新たな攻撃手段を得たことで日和見的に対象を拡大した可能性も。  【攻撃手口の概要】  ・3月26日に観測されたキャンペーンでは、Atlantic Councilを騙るメールからリンクを経由してDarkSwordのリダイレクタ、ローダー、RCE（リモートコード実行）、PACバイパス（ARM系プロセッサのポインタ認証を迂回するコンポーネント）を配信。URLScanの分析でTA446の管理ドメインからの配信が裏付けられている  ・VirusTotalにアップロードされたDarkSwordローダー（MD5: 5fa967dbef026679212f1a6ffa68d575）にはescofiringbijou[.]comへの参照が含まれており、TA446の攻撃インフラ用ドメインと一致  ・TA446が認証情報の窃取と情報収集を目的にDarkSwordを採用したとの推定。アプリの隔離領域を越えてOS全体を制御するサンドボックス脱出までは確認されていない  securityaffairs.com/190139/a…

They stopped trusting platform attribution and built their own source of truth.  First-party tracking, server-side data, real revenue matching. While everyone else optimized toward vanity metrics, these 3 optimized toward what actually moved the bank account.  That's the gap now. It's not strategy. It's not creative. Fundamental Analysis: The "Tale of Two Companies" The Q3 FY26 performance (ended Dec 31, 2025) presents a stark contrast between its domestic operations and its global luxury arm.  The JLR Drag:  The Cyber Shock: The consolidated results were severely impacted by a cyber incident at      JLR, which halted production for nearly a month. This led to a wholesale volume drop of 43.4% YoY to 59,100 units.  Financial Toll: JLR reported an EBIT margin of negative 6.8% and a massive negative free cash flow of £1.5 billion.  The China Problem: JLR is facing structural headwinds in China, its most profitable market. Premium segment demand is shrinking, and intense competition from local new energy vehicles is eroding sales. The Domestic Resilience (The Silver Lining): Market Share Gains: The Indian domestic PV business is thriving. TMPV achieved its  highest-ever quarterly wholesales of ~171,000 units, growing 22% YoY, and      secured the #2 rank with a 13.8% Vahan market share.  EV Dominance:  EV volumes surged 50% YoY, maintaining an exit market share of 46%. The      penetration of EV and CNG models combined stands at a robust 43.6%.  Margin Expansion:  Despite commodity pressures, the domestic business improved its EBITDA  margin to 7.0%, generating a positive Free Cash Flow of ~₹300 Cr.  2. Technical & Valuation Setup  The Valuation Disconnect: TMPV is currently trading at a P/E of 18.6x and   a Price-to-Book ratio of 1.01x. This is exceptionally cheap for a  company with a 20% ROCE and 28.1% ROE. The market is pricing the stock as      if the JLR cyber incident and the China slowdown are permanent, terminal events, rather than temporary or cyclical headwinds.  The Demerger Discount: The recent demerger often creates "orphan  stock" dynamics where institutional algorithms temporarily dump the   spun-off entity. This technical selling pressure has driven the stock down      ~4.6% recently.  3. The Growth Catalysts (FY27 & Beyond) The "Sierra" Effect: TMPV launched the highly anticipated Sierra SUV,   securing over 70,000 bookings on day one. Management confirmed they are      actively ramping up supply chain and in-house capacity at the Sanand 2   factory to meet this massive demand pipeline.  JLR Normalization:  Management confirmed that Q4 production at JLR plants (Solihull and Nitra)  has fully normalized, and the order bank remains strong, particularly for  the Defender model.  Product Pipeline: JLR is preparing for a massive launch cycle, including the Range Rover      Electric and a new production Jaguar, which will shift the narrative from      legacy wind-downs to next-gen electrification.  4. The Risks: JLR Debt:     The consolidated net debt stands at a heavy ~₹39,400 Cr, almost entirely  attributed to JLR. Prolonged weakness in China could strain their      deleveraging timeline.  EV Competition:   While TMPV dominates the Indian EV market, rising competition and changing  subsidy structures (PLI dependence) require constant monitoring  Technical Analysis : The stock has just suffered another violent red month, breaking below the critical 61.8% Fibonacci "Golden Zone" (₹337). The momentum is heavily bearish, meaning you must look lower for the true concrete floor.  Where is the "Generational Buy" Zone? (Institutional Demand)  The Ultimate Target: The ₹260 - ₹290 Zone. Here is the structural logic explaining why institutions will defend this exact box:  The 2023 Macro Order Block:   Look back at your historical data for Q1 2023. Between  January and April 2023, the stock flatlined in a tight, massive accumulation base between ₹262 and ₹289. In April 2023, "Smart  Money" triggered an explosive breakout from this exact box, launching  the stock to its ₹811 ATH. sample_tweets.text: It's funny stuff. When you reside in the United States (and presumably Europe) you always read about the Russian Federation or Chinese government performing cyber state-sponsored operations. It makes us look like a bitch who is getting bullied.  But then you cross the pond on the internet and take a look around and you're like, "hehe ya, we're doing it too actually, our cybersecurity companies just dont discuss it"

Okay, before I make a silly post have some context. Rostelecom is the largest telecommunication company in Russia. If you're in the United States, Rostelecom is basically like their AT&T or Verizon. Anyway, Rostelecom has a Cyber Threat Intelligence division called "Solar Group".  Solar Group releases papers frequently on threats (specifically in the malware domain) targeting the Russian Federation.  I enjoy reading it because, as a person residing in the United States, my Threat Feed is usually threats facing people in the United States (or allies of the United States). Reading threats facing the Russian Federation I'm like, "oh no shit? yall too? lmfao das crazyyyy".  My absolute favorite though is reading papers from Chinese or Russian cybersecurity companies where they accuse the United States government of state-sponsored malware campaigns and the United States government is like, "pfffft? Me? No way, dawg. I'm A CHRISTIAN. You ARE THE BAD GUYS. We go to Church EVERY SUNDAY".  Then both the Russian Federation and Chinese government go like: ">:( u bitch"  But then they do the same thing to us, so it's whatever I guess. We're all doing silly shenanigans on the internet.  An example of the silly shenanigans is a Threat Actor who has compromised various law enforcement agencies in the Russian Federation. This Threat Actor is named "Eagle Werewolf" (what country uses the Eagle?).  Eagle Werewolf has been compromising law enforcement agencies in the Russian Federation, specifically exfiltrating data related to internal case files, active investigations, operational plans from law enforcement agencies, and any information on who the Russian Federation is actively investigating. Eagle Werewolf also appears to be attempting to map internal infrastructure and organization hierarchy (who is who in law enforcement, supervisors, general employees, etc).  That's weird. Why would this mysterious "Eagle Werewolf" want this information? Hmmmmm?
Also, I'm not a European, so I don't understand the humor, but I see a bunch of people blaming Romanians. As an American I do not understand it, but apparently this is big humor for Europeans
March 20th: 15 TONS of gummy candy stolen from semi-trailer in Germany  March 29th: 12 TONS of KitKat bars stolen from Truck leaving Italy en-route to Poland  Who are you people?
I'm not really a humorless piece of poop. I just wanted to do the math on the quantity of KitKats STOLEN.

"I'd eat them".  False. That is impossible.  1 KitKat weights approx. 45g.  1lbs is approx. 450g.  Approx. 10 KitKats in a lbs.  12 tons is 24,000lbs.  That is roughly 240,000 KitKats  1 KitKat is approx. 230 calories.  That is approx. 55,200,000 calories.  The daily recommended calorie intake for an adult is 2,000 calories.  55,200,000 calories is 27,600 days of ideal calorie intake, or roughly 75 years.
What the fuck is going on in Europe?  Who steals 12 TONS of KitKats? What do you even do with that many KitKats?

> wake up > check computer > contacted by teampcp > sends me msbuild and wav payload  its called science

🟠 위협 점수: 8/10 (높음)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 AI를 활용하여 악성코드의 난독화 수준을 높이고 탐지를 회피하는 **DeepLoad**와 같은 지능형 로더를 적극적으로 배포하고 있습니다. 특히 **ClickFix**와 같은 사회공학적 기법을 통해 사용자의 클릭을 유도하여 브라우저 자격 증명을 탈취하거나, 공급망 공격을 통해 신뢰받는 소프트웨어의 취약점을 악용하는 사례가 급증하고 있습니다. 또한, LLM의 코드 생성 능력을 악용하여 조직의 접근 제어 모델을 우회하거나, 취약점을 신속하게 탐색하는 등 공격의 자동화 및 고도화가 두드러지고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **Handala (Krebs on Security, CyberScoop):** 이란 연계 해킹 그룹으로, 최근 FBI 국장 개인 이메일 해킹 및 Stryker 등 의료 기술 기업 대상 Wiper 공격 수행.
*   **TeamPCP (SANS ISC):** 공급망 공격 캠페인을 통해 Databricks 등 주요 기업을 타겟팅하며, 이중 랜섬웨어 운영 및 데이터 유출을 병행.
*   **Silver Fox (WeLiveSecurity):** 일본 기업을 대상으로 세무 및 HR 관련 피싱 이메일을 발송하여 악성코드를 유포하는 캠페인 전개.
*   **Larva-26002 (AhnLab ASEC):** 부적절하게 관리되는 MS-SQL 서버를 타겟으로 ICE Cloud 스캐너를 설치하고 랜섬웨어를 유포하는 공격 수행.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **INC Ransom:** 국내 철강 제조 기업을 대상으로 랜섬웨어 공격을 감행함 (AhnLab ASEC).
*   **국세청 사칭 피싱:** 국세청 세무조사를 사칭한 피싱 메일을 통해 백도어 악성코드를 유포하는 캠페인 확인 (이스트시큐리티 알약).
*   **기타:** 더존비즈온의 글로벌 경영 체제 전환 및 서울 AI 허브의 스타트업 지원 등 기술적 협력 소식 외에 직접적인 대규모 사이버 공격 피해는 보고되지 않음.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-3055:** Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway, Critical, 현재 활발히 악용 중.
*   **CVE-2026-21643:** Fortinet FortiClient EMS, Critical(SQLi), 현재 활발히 악용 중.
*   **CVE-2025-53521:** F5 BIG-IP APM, Critical(RCE), 현재 활발히 악용 중.
*   **CVE-2026-4415:** GIGABYTE Control Center, High(Arbitrary File Write).
*   **CVE-2026-4681:** PTC Windchill PLM, Critical(RCE).

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **CVE:**
    *   CVE-2025-53521 ([DarkReading](https://www.darkreading.com/application-security/fortinet-big-ip-vulnerability-reclassified-rce-exploitation))
    *   CVE-2026-3055 ([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/critical-citrix-netscaler-memory-flaw-actively-exploited-in-attacks/))
    *   CVE-2023-32434, CVE-2023-38606 ([Securelist](https://securelist.com/coruna-framework-updated-operation-triangulation-exploit/119228/))
    *   CVE-2026-4681 ([CISA ICS Advisories](https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/ics-advisories/icsa-26-085-03))
    *   CVE-2008-4302, CVE-2009-3547, CVE-2009-3560 ([NIST NVD](https://web.nvd.nist.gov/view/vuln/detail?vulnId=CVE-2008-4302))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **Citrix 및 F5 장비 패치:** CVE-2026-3055 및 CVE-2025-53521에 대한 최신 보안 패치를 즉시 적용하고, 비정상적인 메모리 덤프 시도가 있는지 로그를 점검하십시오.
*   **ClickFix 대응 교육:** 브라우저에서 '복사/붙여넣기'를 유도하는 ClickFix 사회공학적 기법에 대해 임직원 대상 보안 교육을 실시하고, macOS Tahoe 26.4 이상으로 업데이트를 권장하십시오.
*   **MS-SQL 서버 보안 강화:** 외부 노출된 MS-SQL 서버의 접근 제어를 강화하고, 불필요한 스캐너 설치 여부를 확인하기 위해 엔드포인트 탐지 및 대응(EDR) 솔루션을 점검하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수:** 8/10
